description: Public task corpus for the jcodemunch-mcp token-efficiency benchmark. Tasks are tool-agnostic: any code retrieval tool can be evaluated against the same queries and repos.

repos.description: Express.js — Node.js web framework (~200 source files)
FastAPI — Python async web framework (~150 source files)
Gin — Go HTTP web framework (~80 source files)

tasks.description: Find the core function or method responsible for registering and dispatching route handlers.
tasks.rationale: The most common agent entry point when exploring a web framework codebase.

tasks.description: Find middleware registration, chaining, or execution logic.
tasks.rationale: Middleware is a cross-cutting concern that spans multiple files in every web framework.

tasks.description: Find error handling, exception types, or error propagation logic.
tasks.rationale: Error handling is typically spread across several layers; tests retrieval across abstraction levels.

tasks.description: Find the request/response object definitions or wrappers.
tasks.rationale: Core types referenced throughout the codebase; high symbol density.

tasks.description: Find context object creation, parameter binding, or request context propagation.
tasks.rationale: Tests retrieval of framework-specific patterns that differ significantly across languages.

methodology.baseline: Concatenate all indexed source files and count tokens. This is the minimum token cost for an agent that reads every file before acting.
methodology.jmunch_workflow: call search_symbols (max_results=5), then get_symbol for the top 3 hits. Total = search response tokens + 3 × symbol source tokens.
methodology.note: The baseline is a lower bound — real agents often read files multiple times, so actual savings in production workflows are typically higher.
